Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verbal idiomatic clause with infinitive verb + object
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To endure hardship with courage and emotional control
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects Mongo cultural value of resilience during communal hardship
First Example Sentence: Tango mbula ebebisi bilanga, tata alobaki ete tokanga motema na maboko.
Second Example Sentence: Soki ozwi pasi na mosala, kangá motema na maboko mpe landa liboso.
Third Example Sentence: Bato ya mboka bayekolaka kokanga motema na maboko tango ya mikakatano.

Idiom (Mongo): Komona mokili na miso ya mbula
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verb phrase with metaphorical complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To see life with wisdom gained through experience
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Elders often associated rainfall with time passing and life cycles
First Example Sentence: Nkoko na biso amonaka mokili na miso ya mbula.
Second Example Sentence: Sima ya mibembo mingi, abandi komona mokili na miso ya mbula.
Third Example Sentence: Mobali oyo alobaka malembe mpo amonaka mokili na miso ya mbula.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verb phrase with metaphorical object

Second Example Sentence: Azalaki kolia mokili na matoyi na likita ya mboka.
Third Example Sentence: Soki olingi koyeba makambo, lia mokili na matoyi.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Critique of leaders who promise development but act little

Second Example Sentence: Bato balembi koyoka bato oyo batongaka ndako na maloba.
Third Example Sentence: Na likita, alingaki kotonga ndako na maloba mpo na kozwa maboko.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Metaphor contrasting darkness and sunlight used in oral debates
